svg.title: ESC8 full attack chain — coercion, relay, certificate, DCSync
svg.desc: A flowchart showing the ESC8 NTLM relay attack chain from attacker setup through PetitPotam coercion, NTLM relay to certsrv, PKINIT authentication, and final DCSync domain compromise.
svg.text: NTLM relay to AD CS web enrollment → domain compromise

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What tools do I need to set up for ESC8?')
sendPrompt('How does PetitPotam coerce DC authentication?')
sendPrompt('What is the NTLM NEGOTIATE CHALLENGE AUTHENTICATE flow?')
sendPrompt('What happens when NTLM auth is relayed to certsrv?')
sendPrompt('How does the CA validate the NTLM relay and issue the cert?')
sendPrompt('What is certipy auth and how does PKINIT work?')
sendPrompt('What does the KDC do with a PKINIT request?')
sendPrompt('How does DCSync work with DC machine account credentials?')
sendPrompt('What does DCSync dump and why does DC01$ have replication rights?')
sendPrompt('What can an attacker do with the Administrator NT hash?')
g.text: Pass-the-hash as Administrator → evil-winrm / PtH → full domain control
